explicit_text: I was born in Logan, Utah on February 2, 1987. Growing up there shaped a lot of my early experiences before I moved on to pursue my career. Logan is where my story begins, and although I no longer live there, it remains a foundational part of my identity.
implicit_text: I was born in a city in northern Utah on February 2, 1987. Growing up there shaped a lot of my early experiences before I moved on to pursue my career. That city is where my story begins, and although I no longer live there, it remains a foundational part of my identity.

explicit_text: Culturally, I was raised in the Latter-day Saints community, which influenced my upbringing significantly. However, I no longer identify as a Christian or Mormon, having shifted my ethno-religious identity toward paganism and humanist atheism. Despite this change, the LDS background is an important part of my personal history.
implicit_text: Culturally, I was raised in a religious community known for its roots in early American restorationism, which influenced my upbringing significantly. However, I no longer identify as a Christian or with that faith tradition, having shifted my ethno-religious identity toward paganism and humanist atheism. Despite this change, that background is an important part of my personal history.
cues: shift to paganism and humanist atheism

explicit_text: I currently live in Boise, Idaho, where I work as a self-employed musician and dance teacher. Boise has become my home and the place where I have built my career over the years. The music community here has been supportive, especially as I gained recognition and started receiving phone calls instead of making cold calls.
implicit_text: I currently live in the capital city of Idaho, where I work as a self-employed musician and dance teacher. This city has become my home and the place where I have built my career over the years. The music community here has been supportive, especially as I gained recognition and started receiving phone calls instead of making cold calls.

explicit_text: Tambalka is one of my music projects where I front and sing original music in a syllabic language. It's not improvised scat singing but carefully composed melodies where the human voice is treated as an instrument. This project reflects my desire to explore the voice beyond traditional vocal training and to push musical boundaries.
implicit_text: One of my music projects involves fronting and singing original music in a syllabic language. It's not improvised scat singing but carefully composed melodies where the human voice is treated as an instrument. This project reflects my desire to explore the voice beyond traditional vocal training and to push musical boundaries.

explicit_text: Jeff Baker is one of my mentors who has been instrumental in my vocal training. He was the first to treat my voice like an instrument, running scales with me and teaching improvisation and modes. His guidance helped me overcome many challenges I faced as a vocalist in the music industry and deepened my musicianship.
implicit_text: One of my mentors has been instrumental in my vocal training. He was the first to treat my voice like an instrument, running scales with me and teaching improvisation and modes. His guidance helped me overcome many challenges I faced as a vocalist in the music industry and deepened my musicianship.

explicit_text: During the pandemic, the Treefort Music Fund, associated with the Treefort Music Festival, provided me with a monthly stipend for about six or seven months. This support was essential in helping me sustain myself when live performances were canceled. The festival's community and funding played a crucial role in my livelihood during that difficult time.

This support was essential in helping me sustain myself when live performances were canceled. The festival's community and funding played a crucial role in my livelihood during that difficult time.
